Gender-specific patterns come in an extensive size run.

specs.Zip: Back zip with neck comfort zipper

items.name: Pinnacle Cruiser 3mm
items.blurb: A budget 3mm that borrows features from suits costing twice as much: titanium lining, smooth-skin seals and ankle zips. One of the best value warm-water full suits at dive retailers.
items.review: The Cruiser pairs standard-stretch neoprene through the torso with higher-stretch panels at the arms and legs, so donning is easier than the price suggests. A titanium heat-reflective lining adds warmth without extra bulk, and smooth-skin wrist, ankle and neck seals cut flushing. Ankle zippers and a sturdy back zip speed up changes on a crowded dive deck, and molded shoulder pads keep BCD straps from grinding the neoprene. Sensible across roughly 22-28°C water. At well under half the cost of premium 3mm suits it gives up some suppleness, but nothing that matters on an easy reef week.

specs.Neoprene: Titanium-lined, high-stretch arm and leg panels
specs.Seams: Glued and sewn with smooth-skin seals

thickness_groups.label: 5 mm — temperate / subtropical
thickness_groups.article_intro: 5 mm covers the awkward middle — Mediterranean summers, Pacific thermoclines, the subtropical diving that's too cold for 3 mm and doesn't yet justify 7 mm. The extra couple of millimetres roughly doubles how long you last before the cold registers, and a hood and vest stretch a well-fitted 5 mm down toward 14–15°C. Fit counts for more here than at any other thickness: a suit that gaps at the back flushes cold water with every kick.
tips: Seals make the suit: smooth-skin or glide-skin wrist and ankle cuffs cut the cold-water flush that steals your warmth.
High-stretch neoprene is worth paying for at 5 mm — thicker rubber fights you unless it's engineered to flex.
Add a matching hood and vest for another few degrees; most of your heat is lost through your head and core.

items.name: Bare Reactive 5mm
items.blurb: Graphene-lined full-stretch warmth that reflects your own body heat back at you.
items.review: The Reactive is a 5mm full suit built around Bare's Elastek full-stretch neoprene, so it moves with you on descents and long bottom times without pinching at the shoulders. Its OMNIRED lining uses graphene-infused fibre to capture body heat and return it as infrared, adding several degrees of felt warmth in cooler temperate water. Limestone-based foam, glued-and-blind-stitched seams and reinforced knees make it a durable, warm all-rounder for divers who want premium comfort without a drysuit.

items.name: Fourth Element Proteus II 5mm
items.blurb: The benchmark for warmth in a full-stretch diving suit.
items.review: The Proteus II is widely regarded as one of the warmest 5mm suits you can buy, thanks to a plush thermal lining, a Hydrolock inner neck seal and Glideskin wrist seals that together choke off flushing. It is cut from stretchy neoprene that gives excellent range of motion while keeping a snug, water-tight fit, and the recycled linings reflect the brand's sustainability focus. Divers choose it for prolonged dives in water around 19°C and up where they want maximum comfort and minimal heat loss.

specs.Material: Stretch neoprene with recycled thermal lining
specs.Seams: Glued and blind-stitched, taped stress points
specs.Lining: Thermal plush lining with Hydrolock neck seal

items.name: Scubapro Everflex Yulex 5/4mm
items.blurb: Ultra-flexible plant-based neoprene that feels barely there underwater.
items.review: The Everflex Yulex swaps petroleum foam for natural Yulex rubber, giving a soft, exceptionally stretchy 5/4mm suit that many divers rate as the most mobile in its class. A diagonal front zip and Comfort neck seal reduce flushing while making entry easy, and the fleecy inner lining wicks and warms across a broad temperate range. It is a strong pick for divers who prioritise flexibility and an eco-conscious build without giving up warmth.

items.name: Henderson Thermoprene Pro 5mm
items.blurb: A do-everything 5mm workhorse backed by a 10-year seam warranty.
items.review: The Thermoprene Pro uses Henderson's 250%-stretch neoprene, a big jump over the original, so it pulls on easily and flexes through the full diving range. Seams are minimised, then double-glued and blind-stitched, and a back zip with a leashed pull tab keeps donning simple even solo. Made and warrantied in the USA for a decade, it is the reliable, good-value choice for divers who want durability and warmth without a premium price.

items.name: Aqua Lung Aquaflex 5mm
items.blurb: Four-way stretch and Canadian-style seals for stretch, comfort and warmth.
items.review: The Aquaflex is a long-standing favourite built from four-way stretch neoprene that balances easy movement with solid thermal protection in cooler temperate water. On the 5mm it adds Canadian seals at the wrists and ankles to limit water entry, plus abrasion pads and a comfortable collar for long dives. It is a dependable mid-range suit for divers who want proven warmth and fit without stepping up to flagship money.

specs.Lining: Nylon jersey with Canadian wrist and ankle seals

items.name: Cressi Castoro 5mm
items.blurb: Italian-engineered double-lined warmth at an unbeatable entry price.
items.review: The Castoro is a rugged 5mm double-lined one-piece with a strong YKK back zip and Aquastop under-zip baffle to cut water infiltration. Smooth-neoprene Aquastop seals at the wrists and ankles, pre-shaped legs and anti-wear inserts on the knees and shins make it comfortable to kick and hard to wear out. For divers wanting a warm, durable temperate-water suit on a tight budget, it is one of the best values on the market.

items.name: Bare Nixie Ultra 5mm Women's
items.blurb: A women's-specific 5mm with graphene warmth cut for a true female fit.
items.review: The Nixie Ultra is engineered on a women's-specific pattern, so it fits through the torso, hips and shoulders far better than a unisex suit for less flushing and more comfort. It shares Bare's full-stretch neoprene and OMNIRED graphene lining that reflects body heat back as infrared warmth, with a redesigned collar and ankle zips that make it easy to don and doff. It is the standout choice for women diving temperate and subtropical water who want premium warmth and mobility.

specs.Zip: Back zip with collar zip and ankle zips

items.name: Scubapro Definition Steamer 5mm
items.blurb: A body-mapped 5mm that fits like a glove and insulates like a flagship.
items.review: The Definition Steamer is cut with Scubapro's Body Map System, using 3D tailoring and stretch panels so it hugs the body and cuts internal water movement that steals heat. An Ultraspan chest panel and Stretchtec fabric add mobility, while petroleum-free X-Foam neoprene delivers efficient heat retention rated for a Class B temperate range. Reinforced shoulder, elbow and knee pads and double-zippered seals make it a warm, hard-wearing pick for divers who want a tailored high-end fit.

specs.Material: X-Foam limestone neoprene with Ultraspan and Stretchtec panels

items.name: Beuchat Focea Comfort 6
items.blurb: Sixth generation of Beuchat's flagship one-piece, in 5 mm ultra-flexible Elaskin neoprene with Fireskin thermal lining and the patented Water Dam seal system. The 5 mm men's version covers roughly 18-26 degree C water.
items.review: The Focea Comfort 6 behaves like a semi-dry at a wetsuit price: Water Dam seals at the neck, wrists and ankles cut flushing so the layer of water you warm up stays put through a 50-minute dive. Fireskin lining across the torso speeds up donning and adds noticeable warmth on repetitive days when you climb back into a damp suit. Flex System 2 panels and the Free Flex Zone at the shoulders keep reach unrestricted, so reaching back for a valve or clipping a stage does not fight the neoprene. Supratex 2 kneepads and Black Shield panels take boat-ladder and reef-rubble abrasion without cracking. The preformed anatomical cut removes the water pockets that make straight-cut suits run cold.

specs.Seals: Water Dam at neck, wrists, ankles
specs.Stretch zones: Flex System 2, Free Flex Zone shoulders

items.name: Bare Velocity Ultra 2.0 5mm
items.blurb: Bare's updated full-stretch 5mm gives travelling divers a warm, easy-donning suit for temperate reefs and deep cenote days. The 2.0 revision keeps the Omnired heat-reflective lining and sharpens the anatomical fit.
items.review: The Velocity Ultra 2.0 uses Bare's progressive full-stretch neoprene, so the 5mm panels flex more like a 3mm suit and donning stays quick between dives. Omnired infrared fabric woven through the torso reflects body heat back at the core, a real help on repetitive dives. Seams are glued and blind-stitched, and the heavy-duty back zipper carries a full-length spine pad to block flushing. Comfortable in roughly 16-24°C water; add a hooded vest at the cool end. Men's and women's versions run in a wide size range including short and tall fits.

specs.Neoprene: Progressive full-stretch with Omnired infrared lining

items.name: Fourth Element Xenos ARC 5mm
items.blurb: The current generation of Fourth Element's stretch suit swaps petroleum neoprene for Ocena plant-based natural rubber. A supple 5mm aimed at liveaboard weeks and multi-dive days.
items.review: The Xenos ARC replaces conventional neoprene with foamed natural rubber and lines it inside and out with recycled fabrics, without giving up the easy stretch the original Xenos was known for. Seams are glued and blind-stitched, and the back zip carries a generous flap to limit flushing. Thermal performance sits where a quality 5mm should: comfortable across roughly 16-24°C, covering everything from the Red Sea in winter to the Mediterranean in summer. The trade-off is price, as the natural-rubber construction costs more than comparable neoprene 5mm suits. Men's and women's cuts are offered, with 3mm and 7mm siblings in the same range.

specs.Material: Ocena plant-based natural rubber, recycled linings

items.name: O'Neill Epic 4/3mm
items.blurb: O'Neill's long-running 4/3 fills the awkward gap between a 3mm and a 5mm for divers who run cold. A 4mm core with 3mm limbs keeps warmth where it counts and stretch where you move.
items.review: The Epic is cut entirely from O'Neill's UltraFlex stretch neoprene, with 4mm through the torso and 3mm in the arms and legs. Seams are glued and blind-stitched, and the covert blackout back zip pairs with a double-seal neck closure and lumbar seamless design to keep cold water out of the spine area. It suits roughly 15-21°C water, making it a practical single-suit answer for destinations like the Galapagos in the warm season or southern Australia in summer. The fit is athletic and the wrist and ankle seals are snug, so size carefully. Men's and women's versions are stocked at dive retailers alongside the surf shops.

specs.Zip: Covert blackout back zip, double-seal neck

thickness_groups.label: 7 mm — cold water
thickness_groups.article_intro: 7 mm is about as far as a wetsuit sensibly goes — kelp forests, quarries, temperate reefs, shoulder-season diving in the low teens. It only works if it seals: open wrists, ankles and a loose neck let cold water flush through and strip away the warm layer your body just heated, which is why the better suits use smooth-skin or glued cuffs and a snug collar. Below about 10°C most divers switch to a semi-dry or a drysuit rather than pile on more neoprene.
tips: Sealed or smooth-skin cuffs and a snug neck are non-negotiable at 7 mm — an unsealed suit flushes and you'll get cold fast.
Expect to add weight: thick neoprene is buoyant, so plan your weighting and check trim on the first dive.
A semi-dry with glide-skin seals holds a warmer water layer than a plain 7 mm — worth it if you dive cold often.

items.name: Henderson Thermoprene Pro 7mm
items.blurb: The default-choice cold-water suit: 250% super-stretch neoprene that dives above its price bracket.
items.review: The Thermoprene Pro is a full 7mm one-piece built from ultra-flexible neoprene that stretches to roughly 250%, so it seals close to the body without fighting you when you kit up. Glued-and-blindstitched seams keep flushing low, the back zip runs on a long leash for easy solo donning, and glideskin collar and cuff seals trim the trickle of cold water. It is the suit most divers land on for temperate and cold seas because it balances warmth, mobility and durability without a premium price.

specs.Material: Thermoprene Pro super-stretch neoprene (up to 250% stretch)
specs.Seams: Glued and blindstitched, spot-taped stress points
specs.Lining: Nylon plush interior with glideskin seals

items.name: BARE Reactive 7mm
items.blurb: The warmest suit in BARE's range, with graphene infrared lining that actively kicks back your body heat.
items.review: The Reactive is a premium 7mm full suit built from Elastek full-stretch neoprene laminated to a Graphene Omnired lining, whose infrared-reactive minerals are woven throughout to return radiant body heat and measurably lift core warmth in cold water. The interior runs seamless where seams normally chafe, the underarms are stitch-free for reach, and a long offset-tooth S-Lock back zip with coated tape guards against entry. Divers pick it when they want maximum thermal protection for long or repetitive dives and are willing to pay for the flexibility and warmth.

specs.Material: Elastek full-stretch neoprene with Graphene Omnired laminate
specs.Zip: S-Lock2 offset-tooth back zip with PU-coated tape

items.name: Waterproof W7 7mm
items.blurb: A semi-dry-grade 7mm loaded with seals, pockets and Kevlar knees for divers who want a suit that lasts.
items.review: The W7 is a heavily engineered 7mm full suit that borrows semi-dry thinking: 4-way stretch neoprene keeps it supple while a spine WaterDam and sealed wrist and ankle zips choke off the cold flush that plagues plain wetsuits. It adds practical touches divers actually use, including thigh cargo pockets and Kevlar-reinforced kneepads that shrug off wreck and boat abrasion. Choose it if you dive hard in genuinely cold water and want a durable, well-sealed suit that closes much of the gap to a semi-dry.

specs.Seams: Glued and blindstitched with WaterDam spine baffle
specs.Zip: Back zip plus sealed wrist and ankle zips

items.name: Scubapro Everflex Steamer 7/5mm
items.blurb: A body-mapped 7/5 steamer that keeps thick warmth over the core and thinner stretch where you move.
items.review: The Everflex Steamer runs 7mm over the torso and 5mm at the limbs, a body-mapped layout that concentrates warmth on your core while freeing the arms and legs for easy finning and reaching. Its Diamond Span inner lining drains and channels water, seams are double-blindstitched outside and single inside to cut flushing, and the trim panel count keeps range of motion high. It is a strong pick for cold temperate seas where a diver wants real 7mm warmth without the stiffness of a uniform-thickness suit.

items.name: Aqua Lung Aquaflex 7mm
items.blurb: A supple 4-way-stretch 7mm with dedicated neck and wrist seals that punch above its mid-range price.
items.review: The Aquaflex is a 7mm full suit cut from soft 4-way stretch neoprene, so it hugs the body and stays easy to pull on despite the thickness. Seals at the neck and wrists limit the cold flush that thick suits often suffer, while reinforced kneepads and a back-zip entry keep it practical for regular cold-water use. It seals well and holds up dive after dive without stretching the budget.

specs.Lining: Plush thermal lining with neck and wrist seals

items.name: Cressi Fast 7mm
items.blurb: A budget-friendly one-piece 7mm that delivers honest cold-water warmth without the premium markup.
items.review: The Fast is a straightforward 7mm monopiece built from high-stretch neoprene with a smooth-skin chest and back panel that blocks wind chill on the surface interval and cuts water flow underneath. Glued-and-sewn seams, a back-zip entry and reinforced knees keep it durable, and the fit is trim enough to hold warmth on temperate and cold dives. It is the value pick of the group, a solid choice for a diver who wants real 7mm protection at an entry-level price.

specs.Material: High-stretch neoprene with smooth-skin chest and back panels

items.name: BARE Nixie Ultra 7mm
items.blurb: A women's-specific cold-water 7mm designed around the female form, with infrared lining for real warmth.
items.review: The Nixie Ultra is BARE's flagship women's 7mm, cut on a female-specific pattern so it seals along the back, waist and legs without the bunching that unisex suits leave. It pairs progressive full-stretch neoprene with Omnired infrared lining that reflects body heat back to the core, and a collar-zip donning system makes a thick suit far easier to get into. It is the standout choice for women diving cold water who want a suit that actually fits and keeps them genuinely warm on long dives.

specs.Zip: Back zip with collar-zip donning system

items.name: Henderson Greenprene 7mm
items.blurb: A neoprene-free 7mm built from plant-based foam for divers who want cold-water warmth with a lighter footprint.
items.review: The Greenprene is a 7mm full suit made from a limestone-and-petroleum-free insulating foam derived from natural sources such as sugar cane, plant oils and oyster shells, giving warm, buoyant insulation without conventional neoprene. It keeps the practical Henderson touches divers expect, with glued-and-sewn seams for durability and a back-zip entry with pull leash, and carries a long warranty. Choose it if you want dependable cold-water warmth from a more sustainable material and value ruggedness over ultimate stretch.

items.name: Henderson Aqua Lock 7mm
items.blurb: Henderson's flagship semi-dry for genuinely cold water, built around interlocking seals that mate with matching Aqua Lock hoods, boots and gloves. A drysuit alternative for quarry and cold-coast divers.
items.review: The Aqua Lock pairs 250%-stretch neoprene with a Quick-Dry interior lining, so the suit goes back on warm and nearly dry for a second dive. Every seam is double glued and blind-stitched inside and out, then sealed with liquid-tape coating to shut out water entry. Zipperless interlocking wrist, ankle and neck closures form near-watertight seals on their own or docked to Aqua Lock accessories, and the heavy #10 back zipper sits behind a full dam. In 8-16°C water it is one of the warmest wetsuit options short of a drysuit, and Henderson backs it with a 10-year seam warranty. Men's and women's cuts cover an unusually broad size chart.

specs.Seals: Interlocking zipperless wrist, ankle and neck seals
